title: pseudoliparis uncata
side_effects: Pseudoliparis uncata can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach discomfort due to its toxic compounds. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can lead to vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain, potentially causing dehydration and electrolyte imbalance. Long-term exposure to its toxic alkaloids may also cause kidney and liver damage, as well as an increased risk of bleeding and bruising. Improper use of Pseudoliparis uncata can also interact with certain medications, exacerbating pre-existing health conditions such as high blood pressure and heart disease.
